agent-browser.reason: Only skill documenting the agent-browser CLI itself — 525 lines justified by CLI surface area breadth. Related skills (chrome-devtools, playwright-skill, e2e-validate) reference it but cover different toolchains. No overlap with MEMORY.md/CLAUDE.md.

agent-development.reason: 422 lines covering Claude Code agent authoring spec. Substantial overlap with working-with-cc which covers the same plugin/agent development patterns. Integrate the unique agent-specific YAML frontmatter examples and lifecycle hooks into working-with-cc's agent section.

agent-prompt-evolution.reason: 404 lines of abstract prompt evolution tracking framework with no actionable code examples or CLI commands. No unique content remains.

ai-artist.reason: 129 lines with 129 curated Nano Banana prompts for image generation — unique capability not covered elsewhere. Compact, actionable, and directly invocable.

ai-multimodal.reason: 118 lines covering Gemini API for vision/audio/video analysis and generation. Unique multimodal capability not duplicated by any other skill. Referenced in CLAUDE.md development-rules as a recommended tool.

algorithmic-art.reason: 410 lines with philosophy-first two-phase creative methodology for p5.js generative art. Distinct from canvas-design (static output) and ai-artist (prompt-based). Contains unique conceptual-seed principle and quality-gate patterns.

api-design.reason: 530 lines of production REST API design reference with comprehensive checklist. Covers versioning, pagination, error handling, rate limiting patterns not found in backend-development or other skills.

artifacts-builder.reason: 80 lines covering specific workflow for bundling multi-component React+shadcn/ui apps into single portable HTML for claude.ai. Clear differentiation from web-artifacts-builder (simpler single-file) and frontend-development (general patterns).

ask.reason: 65 lines defining four named advisor personas that are simply Claude roleplaying different roles — no distinct tooling, APIs, or unique logic. All capabilities (trade-off analysis, architecture planning, technical investigation) already covered by brainstorm, plan, research skills and the architect agent. Hardcoded file references to project-specific docs are stale.

backend-development.reason: 104 lines of thin technology decision matrix with no actionable code examples. All patterns covered more thoroughly by api-design (REST patterns), databases skill (data layer), and framework-specific skills (django-patterns, springboot-patterns). No unique content justifies keeping a separate hub skill.

baseline-quality-assessment.reason: Sub-component of the BAIME methodology-bootstrapping skill family (V_meta scoring, iteration convergence). Covers exactly one aspect — iteration 0 baseline quality — already conceptually housed in methodology-bootstrapping (565 lines, the parent framework). Entirely self-referential to BAIME/Bootstrap experiment series with no relevance to general software development. The 465-line skill could be reduced to a short section within methodology-bootstrapping covering 'invest in iteration 0 for faster convergence' with the scoring table.

beautiful-mermaid.reason: Well-structured, actionable skill for rendering Mermaid diagrams to SVG/PNG via Beautiful Mermaid library with themed output and 4K screenshot capture. Clear differentiation from 3 related Mermaid skills: mermaidjs-v11 (syntax reference only), mermaid-tools (batch extraction), diagram (DDD methodology). Only skill covering the full render pipeline. Technical references current (Mermaid v11, bun/tsx/deno runtimes).

better-auth.reason: Better Auth is the actual auth framework used by SessionForge (confirmed in MEMORY.md). Provides actionable reference: installation, server/client setup, plugin matrix, implementation checklist. References version 2.0.0 which is current. No other skill covers Better Auth specifically. Well-scoped to a single technology with practical code snippets.

blog-post-writer.reason: Unique, actionable skill with clear purpose: transforms brain dumps into blog posts in a specific personal voice (Nick Nisi). References bundled resources (voice-tone.md, story-circle.md) for style guidance. Differentiated from copywriting, technical-content-creator, humanize-blog-post by its specific voice/persona focus. No outdated technical references.

bootstrap.reason: Thin orchestrator chaining plan and cook skills with three greenfield-specific phases (Git init, tech stack research, design). Issues: (1) 4 workflow reference files plus shared-phases.md create deep token cost chain — consider inlining into SKILL.md. (2) Mode flags (--full/--auto/--fast/--parallel) duplicate flags on plan and cook, creating pass-through layer. (3) No version field. Recommendation: consolidate workflow references into SKILL.md, remove redundant flag pass-through, add version field.

brainstorm.reason: Well-scoped ideation/advisory skill with distinct niche: structured trade-off analysis and multi-option evaluation before implementation. Clear 8-phase process with handoff to plan skill. Properly differentiates from ask (consultation), plan (implementation), pdd (design docs). References to AskUserQuestion, planner agent, WebSearch are all current. 84 lines, appropriate size.

brand-guidelines.reason: Compact (80 lines) self-contained reference storing Anthropic's official brand hex codes, typography (Poppins/Lora), and color-application rules. No overlap with MEMORY.md/CLAUDE.md. Provides shared design-token reference consumed by pptx, canvas-design, technical-content-creator, copywriting, pdf skills. Factual content does not go stale.

build-quality-gates.reason: At 1853 lines, second-largest skill in inventory. Most content is generic CI boilerplate bash scripts and procedural walkthrough text any LLM can produce on demand. Tightly coupled to niche BAIME framework with custom metrics (V_instance, V_meta) — four other BAIME-family skills share same vocabulary. References experiment-config.json suggesting project-specific rather than general use. CI/CD content overlaps with deployment-patterns (434 lines) and production-readiness-audit (759 lines). Go/Make examples irrelevant to current Next.js/bun project.

building-native-ui.reason: Highly specialized Expo Router native UI skill with 14 detailed reference files covering cutting-edge APIs (iOS 26 liquid glass, SDK 54+ NativeTabs, Link.Preview/Link.Menu). Distinct from expo-development (build tooling), react-native-expert (React Navigation), react-native-skills (checklist), mobile-development (platform decisions). Very current content (2025+). Dense, actionable guidance expensive to reconstruct.

No substantial overlap — ai-artist uses external APIs, algorithmic-art uses p5.js, frontend-design covers web UI. Fills distinct niche of museum/magazine-quality static visual design. blog-writing-guide.reason: Sentry-specific voice, banned language list, and editorial standards are highly opinionated and unique. Valuable as a domain-specific writing style guide.

chrome-devtools.reason: Comprehensive Puppeteer-based browser automation with persistent sessions, ARIA snapshots, auth methods, and debugging. Unique capability not duplicated elsewhere.

ck-help.reason: Meta-skill for discovering and navigating other skills. Lightweight, unique, serves as a routing layer with Python-based intent matching.

claude-api.reason: Comprehensive, current (Feb 2026 model table), covers Claude API + Agent SDK across 8 languages. Decision tree and pitfalls highly actionable.

subagent-driven-development.reason: Good pattern for sequential task execution with fresh subagents. References deprecated TodoWrite (should be TaskCreate/TaskUpdate) and TDD which conflicts with no-mocks mandate. Update tool references.

subagent-prompt-construction.reason: Unique methodology for compact subagent prompts using lambda contracts. However reads more like formal spec than actionable guidance. Add plain-English quick-start section. Symbolic notation should supplement, not replace.

supabase.reason: Comprehensive actionable Supabase reference covering Edge Functions, declarative schema, migrations, DB functions, and RLS policies. Well-scoped. Not replaceable. Valuable for Supabase projects.

tailwind-v4-shadcn.reason: Production-tested Tailwind v4 + shadcn/ui setup with specific v4 gotchas (tw-animate-css deprecation, @theme inline pattern). Prevents real documented errors. Current version references.

tanstack.reason: Focused TanStack Start/Form/AI reference with server functions, middleware, route patterns. Clearly scoped with applicability guard. Not duplicated elsewhere.

tdd-workflow.reason: Directly contradicts no-mock validation mandate enforced by block-test-files.js hook. Creates test files, uses jest.mock(). Use functional-validation and e2e-validate instead.

team.reason: Core orchestration skill for Agent Teams with 4 templates (research, cook, review, debug). Detailed execution protocols, CK context blocks, token budgets. Unique multi-agent coordination engine.

technical-content-creator.reason: Comprehensive technical blog writing with Claude-native visual generation (Mermaid, SVG, HTML cards). Unique combination not replaceable by blog-post-writer or copywriting.

technical-debt-management.reason: Rigorous SQALE methodology with quantified formulas, code smell taxonomy, value-effort matrix. Language-agnostic. Unique systematic debt quantification.

template-skill.reason: Empty placeholder with only 'Insert instructions below'. Zero actionable content. skill-creator already covers skill creation workflow.

test.reason: Test execution and QA reporting skill useful for running existing test suites. Needs explicit applicability guard for no-mock projects — currently references frameworks that conflict with functional-validation mandate.

testing-anti-patterns.reason: Documents mock-based testing anti-patterns — irrelevant when mandate is NEVER write mocks. Skill's own applicability guard says 'DO NOT USE when project has no-mock mandate.' functional-validation covers the same ground correctly.

testing-cc-plugins-python-sdk.reason: References ANTHROPIC_API_KEY setup which violates SessionForge SDK auth rules and is blocked by hook. Python SDK API may have changed. Needs update to reflect current SDK and remove API key references.

testing-skills-with-subagents.reason: Unique meta-skill for bulletproofing skill documentation using RED-GREEN-REFACTOR applied to process docs. Pressure scenario methodology is original and actionable.

testing-strategy.reason: Go-specific testing methodology with explicit applicability guard stating 'DO NOT USE when project has no-mock mandate' and 'not Go-based.' SessionForge is TypeScript/Next.js with no-mock mandate.

theme-factory.reason: 10 curated themes with color palettes and font pairings for styling artifacts. Unique pre-built visual identities with PDF showcase. Lightweight and focused.

threejs.reason: Comprehensive Three.js skill with 556 searchable examples, search CLI tool, progressive reference files. Strong applicability guard. Actionable. Unique.

title-only-detection-iteration.reason: Hyper-specific to a single video file (android-12-3-debug.mp4) for YouTube Shorts detection project. References specific frame directories and Python modules. Zero transferability.

transform-validation-prompt.reason: Transforms ungated prompts into validation-gated prompts with blocking PASS/FAIL criteria. Unique meta-skill operationalizing the no-mock Iron Rule into prompt engineering.

ui-review-tahoe.reason: Specialized macOS 26 Tahoe review covering Liquid Glass design system, HIG compliance, SwiftUI/AppKit patterns. Timely for macOS 26. Not duplicated by web-focused UI skills.

ui-styling.reason: Significant overlap with ui-ux-pro-max: both cover shadcn/ui, Tailwind CSS, responsive design, dark mode. Core content is a subset. Merge canvas design system reference and utility scripts into ui-ux-pro-max.

ui-ux-pro-max.reason: Most comprehensive UI/UX design intelligence skill with searchable database (50 styles, 97 palettes, 57 font pairings, 99 UX guidelines). Unique search-based recommendation engine.

usage-based-automation.reason: Unique meta-skill for mining Claude Code session history to generate project-specific commands, agents, and skills. Actionable bash commands for extracting patterns from JSONL session files.

use-mcp.reason: Depends on Gemini CLI as primary execution path which may not be installed. Improve by making Gemini dependency optional, strengthening fallback path with concrete MCP tool discovery steps.

using-git-worktrees.reason: Duplicates worktree skill which has proper Node.js script with monorepo support. Merge .gitignore safety verification and directory priority logic into worktree as pre-creation checklist.

vercel-deploy-claimable.reason: Unique deployment capability — deploys to Vercel without auth via deploy script. Framework auto-detection and troubleshooting for network egress are actionable.

verification-before-completion.reason: Strong discipline-enforcement but heavily overlaps with gate-validation-discipline and evidence-gate-reminder.js hook. Trim to focus on unique Iron Law framing and rationalization prevention table, reduce to ~80 lines.

visual-inspection.reason: Comprehensive visual QA protocol with universal checklist, severity classification, and platform-specific routing. Unique — no other skill covers systematic screenshot review.

watzup.reason: Simple focused utility — reviews current branch changes and summarizes session work without implementing anything. Lightweight, clear trigger, valuable 'do not implement' guard.

web-artifacts-builder.reason: Unique skill for self-contained HTML artifacts using React 18 + shadcn/ui + Parcel bundling into single HTML. Anti-AI-slop design guidance.

web-design-guidelines.reason: Lean skill (44 lines) that fetches Vercel web-interface-guidelines from GitHub. Actionable with concrete source URL and clear review workflow. Complements visual-inspection.

web-frameworks.reason: Comprehensive Next.js + Turborepo guide but references local Python scripts that may not exist. Add guard for missing scripts, trim RemixIcon content that inflates the skill.

web-testing.reason: Contradicts core mandate: NEVER write mocks, stubs, test doubles, unit tests, or test files. Entirely about Playwright E2E tests, Vitest unit tests, k6 load tests — all blocked by hooks.

webapp-testing.reason: Python Playwright scripting for local app testing — aligns with functional validation mandate (real browser interaction, not test files). Does not create test files.

working-with-cc.reason: Authoritative reference library for Claude Code internals — 36 reference docs covering plugins, hooks, skills, MCP, settings. Canonical Claude Code documentation source.

worktree.reason: Well-structured worktree skill with Node.js script supporting monorepo detection, branch prefix inference, slug generation. Superior to using-git-worktrees.

writing-plans.reason: Good plan-writing skill but TDD references throughout conflict with no-test-files mandate. Replace TDD step patterns with functional-validation patterns.

writing-skills.reason: Meta-skill for creating Claude Code skills using TDD-for-documentation methodology. Comprehensive: CSO optimization, frontmatter spec, token efficiency. Canonical skill-authoring reference.

xc-mcp.reason: Comprehensive iOS development automation for XC-MCP server. Well-structured with routing table, progressive disclosure, applicability guard. Self-contained for iOS/macOS projects.

xlsx.reason: Deep production-quality spreadsheet skill covering openpyxl + pandas workflows, financial model color coding, formula construction rules. Uniquely valuable.

yaml-compliance.reason: YAML frontmatter spec already documented in working-with-cc references. This 164-line skill duplicates that reference. Merge validation checklist and quick-fix patterns into working-with-cc.
